title: Register a non-profit company
description: Set up a Barbadian non-profit company under the Companies Act, Cap. 308.
summary: A non-profit company is a company that exists for a charitable, religious, educational or other public-good purpose. It is registered under the Companies Act, Cap. 308, but a different declaration form is used.

The key difference between a non-profit and a regular company is what happens with any money or assets it has. A non-profit must use them for its stated purpose – they cannot be paid out to members as profit.

Common examples include charities, religious organisations, sports clubs, professional associations and community groups. If your activity is for a public good and you do not plan to distribute profits, this is usually the right structure.
Like other companies, a non-profit needs a Barbadian attorney-at-law to sign the declaration. The fees are usually lower than for a regular for-profit company.

items: the proposed name of the company
the charitable or public-good purpose of the company
the address of the registered office
the names, addresses and dates of birth of every director
the names of every founding member

items.name: Articles of incorporation for a non-profit company
Declaration form signed by your attorney-at-law
Notice of directors and registered office
Proof of payment of the registration fee

fees: There is a reduced incorporation fee for non-profit companies. Confirm at CAIPO.
how_long: A decision usually takes 5 to 10 working days.

options.heading: Through an attorney-at-law
content: A Barbadian attorney-at-law will prepare the documents and file them with CAIPO.

contact.agency: Corporate Affairs and Intellectual Property Office (CAIPO)
contact.address:
Baobab Tower, Ground Floor
Warrens
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘A reduced incorporation fee for non-profit companies’ is a common practice in Caribbean company registries. Exact fees should be confirmed with CAIPO.
‘A decision usually takes 5 to 10 working days’ is an estimate.
